current_task.scope: Make GPT Pro / ChatGPT Apps direction review a product-strategy review path, keep code review on Codex native reviewer subagents, wire docs_researcher into subagent planning, and make selfdex doctor validate the .codex subagent policy surface.
current_task.verification_target: focused subagent planner and setup doctor tests, full unittest discovery, plugin validation, doc drift, campaign boundary check, commit gate check, CLI/npm smoke, reviewer subagent, GitHub post-push check

orchestration_profile.trigger_basis: The user approved applying the split where GPT Pro / ChatGPT Apps direction review is for project direction and Codex native reviewer subagents handle code review, then explicitly requested commit and push.
orchestration_profile.subagent_permission: @selfdex invocation is explicit standing permission for Selfdex to recommend and use Codex native subagents when useful.
subagent_limits: Read-only explorer, reviewer, docs_researcher, CI/log analysis, and summarization lanes may be used automatically after @selfdex is invoked.
Write-capable worker lanes require a frozen contract and disjoint write boundary.
Commit, push, publish, deploy, production writes, secret access, destructive filesystem operations, and destructive Git operations still require separate explicit approval.
orchestration_profile.selection_reason: The previous policy made the GPT Pro / ChatGPT Apps direction-review path ambiguous and reviewer feedback found two valid readiness gaps: docs_researcher was not reachable from planner output, and selfdex doctor did not validate .codex subagent setup.

contract_freeze: @selfdex is the user-facing invocation for Selfdex command-center mode.
Calling @selfdex means the user explicitly permits Selfdex to use official Codex native Subagents/MultiAgentV2 when useful.
Selfdex must use official Codex terms in active policy: main agent, subagent, agent thread, explorer, worker, reviewer, docs_researcher.
Selfdex must stop presenting single-session, delegated-*, score_total, and agent_budget as active runtime controls.
Read-only subagents can run after @selfdex when useful; write-capable workers still need frozen write boundaries.
GPT Pro / ChatGPT Apps direction review is for product direction, product fit, improvement ideas, and additional features.
Code diff review stays with Codex native reviewer subagents.
docs_researcher must be reachable from planner recommendations when official docs, API, SDK, MCP, ChatGPT Apps, OpenAI, model, or GPT behavior matters.
selfdex doctor must fail or warn clearly when the project-scoped .codex subagent policy files are missing or stale.

reviewer.reviewer_target: official subagent permission boundary, removed legacy topology vocabulary, plugin validator coverage
reviewer.reviewer_focus: Do active docs imply uncontrolled autonomy? Do they keep explicit approvals for risky actions?
reviewer.reviewer_result: Codex native reviewer subagent found no P0/P1 findings. Two P2 findings were repaired inside scope: planner recommendations now preserve docs_researcher/reviewer for guarded docs-heavy candidates, and selfdex doctor now checks worker policy content beyond the role name.

last_update.status: Review-routing and subagent-readiness follow-up implemented, reviewer findings repaired, verified, and recorded.
verification_result: python -m unittest discover -s tests: pass, 223 tests
python -m unittest tests.test_plan_subagent_fit tests.test_plan_next_task tests.test_selfdex_setup tests.test_commit_gate: pass, 30 tests
npm.cmd pack --dry-run --json: pass after sandbox escalation
git diff --check: pass with CRLF warnings only

retrospective.actual_agent_usage: main implementation plus Codex native reviewer subagent.
retrospective.next_gate_adjustment: Future @selfdex sessions should route product/app direction review through GPT Pro / ChatGPT Apps when user-approved, keep code diff review on reviewer subagents, and keep .codex policy files under setup doctor checks.
